notes.note: Lucius Aemilius Paullus, consul 50 BC and elder brother of the triumvir-to-be Lepidus; Cicero meets him at Caieta and learns from him both the news of the pseudo-Marius and a warning that Cassius's life was threatened.

Again the impostor "Marius" (Herophilus/Amatius); the "thoroughly wretched news" is presumably of his execution by Antony and the popular unrest it caused. No registry entity matches the pseudo-Marius.

notes.anchor: on the seventeenth day before the Kalends
notes.note: Roman dates count inclusively backward from fixed points; "the seventeenth before the Kalends (of May)" is 15 April 44 BC. Cicero dates several of these post-Ides letters by such reckonings.
Young Marcus Cicero, then about to study at Athens; Cicero, pleased by the boy's polished Greek letter (Greek pinos, "polish"), urges Atticus to keep him amply supplied.
